summary: The run/generation/candidate/agenome state machines are coherent for the happy path and the basic terminal set is sound (terminal-state-immutability and "no spend after spent/failed/culled" invariants are present). But several failure and edge transitions the project's own flows and test plan explicitly name are missing from the actual state diagrams: the structured-output REPAIR path is named in the test inventory but the candidate machine treats invalid as a one-shot dead end; there is no generation transition for zero-survivors / all-culled mid-run (the diagram always flows into reproducing); partial-generation failure (some agenomes fail, generation continues) has no bridging rule; novelty scoring has no explicit placement in either the generation or candidate states; and there is no escape hatch for a wall-clock/stuck run despite the cap being a load-bearing invariant. The Agenome state machine is also absent from ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT §7 (present only in DOMAIN_MODEL), creating a single-source-of-truth gap for the runtime kernel that owns it. None of these contradict locked decisions; they are underspecification of failure transitions.

findings.finding: The generation state machine has no zero-survivors / all-culled transition. The only paths are 'verifying -> scoring -> reproducing -> completed' (always assumes survivors to reproduce) and 'pending -> running -> failed'. USER_FLOWS explicitly names 'No candidates survive' as a failure state of the Score/Cull/Fuse flow, and DOMAIN_MODEL names 'all candidates are culled' as a run-failure cause, but there is no state edge for it. A generation where everything is culled cannot legally reach 'completed' (nothing to reproduce) nor 'failed' (no execution error occurred), so the machine is stuck mid-run.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §7 (Generation states) and DOMAIN_MODEL.md §State Machines (Generation State); cross-ref USER_FLOWS.md 'Flow: Score, Cull, Fuse, And Mutate' Failure States ('No candidates survive') and DOMAIN_MODEL.md 'Lifecycle Success / Failure States'
findings.proposed_fix: Add an explicit zero-survivors edge, e.g. 'scoring -> completed' (generation completes with no offspring) plus a run-level rule: if a completed generation has zero eligible parents AND run caps would otherwise continue, the run transitions 'running -> completing -> completed' (best-prior-idea preserved) or 'running -> failed' (no surviving idea at all). Emit a dedicated zero-survivors event so the dashboard can explain it.

findings.finding: The structured-output REPAIR path is named as a deliverable but absent from the candidate state machine. The model-gateway 'Return accepted or rejected structured result' and the test inventory lists an 'invalid output repair/reject path', implying a repair-then-revalidate loop. But the candidate machine only has 'created -> invalid' as a terminal dead end with no transition back to 'created'/'under_review' after a successful repair, and no bounded retry counter. As written, any candidate that fails first-pass structured validation is permanently invalid with no repair attempt.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §7 (Candidate states: 'created -> invalid') vs ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §6 model-gateway ('Return accepted or rejected structured result') and the test inventory line 'invalid output repair/reject path' (ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md test section, ~L461); USER_FLOWS.md 'Execute Generation Lifecycle' Failure States ('Agenome fails to produce structured output')
findings.proposed_fix: Make the repair path a first-class transition: 'created -> repairing -> under_review' on successful repair, 'repairing -> invalid' after N failed repair attempts (N capped, default 1 retry for MVP). Record repair attempts as energy-metered events so the repair loop is bounded by the energy ledger and cannot loop unbounded.

findings.finding: Partial-generation failure (some agenomes fail, others succeed) has no bridging rule. The agenome machine has 'active -> failed' per-agenome and the generation machine has an all-or-nothing 'running -> failed', but nothing specifies the threshold at which individual agenome failures roll up to a failed generation versus the generation proceeding with the survivors. USER_FLOWS names 'Agenome fails to produce structured output' and 'Provider timeout or rate limit' as expected per-agenome failures during generation execution, so partial failure is the common case, not an edge case.
findings.evidence: DOMAIN_MODEL.md §State Machines (Agenome State 'seeded -> active -> failed'; Generation State 'pending -> running -> failed') and USER_FLOWS.md 'Execute Generation Lifecycle' Failure States
findings.proposed_fix: State the rule explicitly: a generation proceeds to 'verifying' as long as at least one candidate reached 'created' (or a configurable min-population-survival threshold); the generation only takes 'running -> failed' if ALL agenomes fail or provider failures exceed the run's retry cap. Emit a partial-failure event capturing failed agenome ids so the dashboard distinguishes 'some died' from 'generation failed'.

findings.finding: Novelty scoring has no explicit placement in the generation or candidate state machines. Novelty is a locked MVP fitness component, but the generation machine jumps 'verifying -> scoring' with no novelty step, and the candidate machine goes 'checked -> scored' with novelty invisible. It is ambiguous whether novelty is computed inside 'scoring' (selection-scoring owns it per §6), during 'verifying', or as a separate sub-phase — and where the embedding call (a provider call that consumes energy and can fail/timeout) is metered and what happens if it fails.
findings.evidence: DOMAIN_MODEL.md §State Machines (Generation 'verifying -> scoring'; Candidate 'checked -> scored') and §Core Entities (NoveltyScore); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §6 selection-scoring ('Owns scoring policy, novelty score...') vs the Generation/Candidate state diagrams which omit a novelty state
findings.proposed_fix: Document that novelty is computed within the 'scoring' generation state (owned by selection-scoring) as a fitness input, and specify the degrade path: if the embedding provider call fails or pgvector is unavailable, novelty defaults to a neutral score (or app-level cosine fallback per §9) and fitness is still computed — the generation must not get stuck in 'scoring' on a novelty-call failure. Add a novelty-skipped/degraded event for auditability.

findings.finding: No escape hatch for a stuck or wall-clock-exceeded run. Run caps include a wall-clock timeout (locked invariant) and the kill switch is load-bearing, but no state transition fires on timeout/stall. A generation stuck in 'verifying' (e.g. a critic call hangs) or 'scoring' has no timeout-driven edge to 'failed'/'stopped'. The state machines model graceful stop ('running -> stopping -> stopped') but not the runtime forcibly terminating a hung generation.
findings.evidence: DOMAIN_MODEL.md §Business Rules ('Run caps include at least ... wall-clock timeout') and §Invariants; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §6 runtime-kernel ('cap enforcement ... terminal state'); USER_FLOWS.md 'Execute Generation Lifecycle' Failure States ('Runtime kills runaway execution') and 'Stop Or Complete A Run' ('Stop is a hard control path, not best-effort UI state') — but no wall-clock edge in any state diagram
findings.proposed_fix: Add timeout/abort edges from every non-terminal generation state ('running'/'verifying'/'scoring'/'reproducing') -> generation 'failed', and a run-level 'any non-terminal -> failed' fired by the wall-clock cap or kill switch. Define a per-state deadline so the kernel can detect a stalled state, not just total run time. Emit an aborted-by-cap event distinct from a clean stop.

findings.finding: The Agenome state machine is missing from ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT §7. The architecture draft documents Run, Generation, and Candidate states but omits Agenome states ('seeded -> active -> spent -> eligible_parent', etc.), which exist only in DOMAIN_MODEL. Since runtime-kernel is the authoritative owner of the agenome lifecycle and §7 is the data/state contract, the binding artifact under-specifies a machine the kernel must enforce, risking drift between the two docs.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §7 (lists Run/Generation/Candidate states only) vs DOMAIN_MODEL.md §State Machines (Agenome State present)
findings.proposed_fix: Copy the Agenome state machine into ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT §7 so all four machines live in the binding state contract, and add the missing 'spent -> eligible_parent' qualifier (an agenome should only become eligible_parent if its candidate(s) reached a fitness score, per DOMAIN_MODEL's 'cannot be selected for parent contribution until it has a fitness score').

findings.finding: The candidate 'scored -> culled' transition is the only cull edge, but a 'selected' (winner-contributing) candidate whose lineage later loses can never be culled, and a 'rejected'/'invalid' candidate has no defined relationship to culling. It is unclear whether culling operates on candidates, agenomes, or both, and whether a selected candidate from generation N can be superseded/retired in generation N+1.
findings.evidence: DOMAIN_MODEL.md §State Machines (Candidate 'scored -> culled'; Agenome 'eligible_parent -> culled') and §Core Entities (CullingEvent 'target ids')
findings.proposed_fix: Clarify that CullingEvent targets agenome lineages (the cull unit) and that candidate 'culled' is a derived status reflecting its agenome being culled; document that 'selected' is per-generation and a prior winner can be superseded without being 'culled' (it remains the best-of-its-generation for replay). No new state needed, just a note resolving the candidate-vs-agenome cull granularity.

findings.finding: When a run ends via all-culled / zero-survivors, should it be classified terminal-'failed' or terminal-'completed-with-no-winner'? DOMAIN_MODEL lists 'all candidates are culled' under failed-run causes, but USER_FLOWS 'Stop Or Complete A Run' says the system 'selects best surviving idea if one exists' and emits run-completed OR run-failed — leaving the no-survivor classification ambiguous. This affects dashboard semantics and the success-metric definition for the showcase.
findings.evidence: DOMAIN_MODEL.md §Lifecycle Success/Failure States ('A failed run may end because all candidates are culled') vs USER_FLOWS.md 'Stop Or Complete A Run' Step 4-5 ('selects best surviving idea if one exists ... emits run-completed or run-failed')
findings.proposed_fix: Decision needed: terminal status for a run that produced candidates but retained zero survivors. Recommended default for MVP: if at least one candidate was ever 'selected' in any generation (a best-so-far existed), end as 'completed' with that as the final idea even if the final generation culled out; only end as 'failed' if NO generation ever produced a scored survivor. This keeps the demo's 'best idea' panel populated in the common case.
